explanation:
Personal finance and cryptocurrency investing leverage universal mathematical principles (compound interest, time value of money, risk-return optimization). This calculator applies standard financial industry formulas.

## How it works

Multi-cryptocurrency to ARS conversion.

This calculator applies the formula using your input values, checking typical ranges and alerting you to any unreasonable figures.

## Typical benchmarks by time horizon

| Horizon | S&P 500 | Bonds (10y) | Cash |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 year | Highly variable | 4–5% | 3–5% |
| 5 years | ~8% average | ~4% | Inflation rate |
| 10 years | ~9–10% | ~4% | Below inflation |
| 20+ years | ~10% nominal | ~5% | N/A |

## Cryptocurrency investment principles

- **Don't abandon your plan** during market downturns. Emotion is the worst advisor.
- **Diversify**: avoid concentrating in a single asset, sector, or region.
- **Keep costs low**: annual fees above 1% will consume 30% of your returns over 30 years.
- **Think long-term**: cryptocurrency works better over 10+ years, not months.
- **Rebalance annually**: once per year, no more frequently.

## Final notes

This calculation is an informational reference. For critical decisions (financial, medical, legal), consult a qualified professional. Values are current to 2026 and updated periodically.

faq.q: How do I calculate my total crypto portfolio value in USD?
faq.a: Enter the quantity of each coin (BTC, ETH, USDT) and the current USD price for each. The calculator multiplies quantity × price for each coin and sums them.

faq.q: Should I diversify across Bitcoin, Ethereum, and stablecoins?
faq.a: Yes. Bitcoin and Ethereum are volatile but offer growth potential. Stablecoins like USDT reduce volatility and provide a cash equivalent. Holding a mix is a common strategy.

faq.q: What exchange rate should I use when converting crypto to local currency?
faq.a: For Argentine users, the MEP (Mercado Electrónico de Pagos) rate is standard. Use the current MEP rate in the calculator. Rates change continuously, so check your exchange for real-time prices.

faq.q: Do I have to report cryptocurrency conversions on my taxes?
faq.a: Yes, in most jurisdictions including Argentina. Converting crypto to fiat (or crypto-to-crypto) triggers a taxable event. Consult a tax professional for your specific situation.

faq.q: How should I backup my cryptocurrency wallet securely?
faq.a: Keep a physical, encrypted copy of your seed phrase or private keys. Store it offline (paper or hardware wallet), separate from your device. Never share this information.

faq.q: Is it safer to keep crypto in an exchange wallet or personal wallet?
faq.a: Personal (self-custodial) wallets give you full control but require security discipline. Exchange wallets are convenient but you trust the exchange. For large holdings, a hardware wallet is recommended.

faq.q: What historical returns can I expect from cryptocurrency?
faq.a: Bitcoin and Ethereum have shown extreme volatility. Past returns don't predict future results. Traditional stocks average ~10% annually; crypto is far more unpredictable. Invest only what you can afford to lose.

faq.q: How often should I update my portfolio value calculation?
faq.a: Check when prices move significantly or when you add/remove coins. Weekly checks are common for active investors; less frequent for long-term holders. Avoid obsessive daily tracking, which can lead to poor decisions.
